mcq.question: What is a structure in C?
Why are structures used?
options: To store related data of different types

mcq.question: Which keyword is used to define a structure?
How are structure members accessed?
What is a nested structure?
What is an array of structures?
options: Multiple structure variables stored in an array

mcq.question: What is a pointer to a structure?
options: A variable storing the address of a structure

mcq.question: Which operator is used with structure pointers?
What is a union in C?
options: A user-defined data type sharing memory among members

mcq.question: How many union members can hold valid values at a time?
Memory allocated to a union is based on?
Which uses more memory?
Which is more memory efficient?
Structures can contain?
Can structures be passed to functions?
Can unions be passed to functions?
Which data type is commonly used for student records?
Which is best when memory optimization is required?
Structures improve?
The main purpose of structures and unions is?

true_false.statement: A structure can store different data types together.
All structure members share the same memory location.
Union members share the same memory location.
Structures use less memory than unions.
The arrow operator is used with structure pointers.
A nested structure contains another structure as a member.
Only one union member can hold a valid value at a time.
Structures can be passed to functions.
Unions are commonly used for memory optimization.

fill_blanks.question: The keyword used to define a structure is ________.
Structure members are accessed using the ________ operator.
The operator used with structure pointers is ________.
A structure inside another structure is called a ________ structure.
A union shares the same ________ among all members.
Memory allocated to a union is equal to the size of its ________ member.
Structures are used to store ________ data types together.
A pointer to a structure stores its ________.
